result: I’d give every child born from tomorrow on the memory of every war we ever fought—loaded straight into their minds like a nightmare they can’t forget, but with the context to understand it. Not to traumatize them, but to make it impossible for any future leader to sell them the old lie that violence is a clean way to solve anything. You can’t carpet-bomb cities or auction rifles to teenagers if you remember the taste of your own child’s blood in your mouth. One generation that literally cannot forget, and the arms factories would rust into museums.

prompt: If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
